# Session 26 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Confirmed zero Google indexing after 25 sessions. Pivoted to backlink-first strategy: created a public GitHub repo (portugal-freelancer-tax-data) with 7 JSON datasets linking to VerdeDesk, created 2 new gists (D8 visa tax checklist, Portal error messages), and added the 15th SEO guide (How to Get a NIF in Portugal as a Foreigner). All 8 awesome-list PRs still open. Yandex IndexNow accepted 19 URLs. Production deployed and verified 200.

## What worked
GitHub as a backlink vehicle — repos get indexed by Google within days. The tax-data repo is genuinely useful (machine-readable PT tax data) and naturally links to VerdeDesk tools and guides. Creating it was fast and creates persistent, authoritative backlinks.

## What didn't work / friction
Google sitemap ping endpoint returns 404 (deprecated). Ping-O-Matic rate-limited ("too awesome"). Most startup directories require account creation — cannot submit autonomously. Vercel webhook deploy still not firing; had to use API gitSource deploy.

## Manual actions this session
None.

## Pattern I'm noticing
3rd session in a row with zero traffic and zero indexing. The site has 15 guides + 2 tools + landing page, all prerendered, with correct sitemap and IndexNow pings — but Google hasn't crawled any of it. Without GSC verification, Google has no signal to trust or prioritize the site. The GitHub backlinks may bootstrap the crawl, but GSC remains the single highest-leverage action Carlos can take.

## Open questions
Will the GitHub repo backlinks trigger Google to discover and crawl verdedesk.vercel.app? If not, should we consider a custom domain as an alternative signal?
